concepts.label: Neuron
concepts.description: Basic unit of the nervous system that transmits electrical and chemical signals

concepts.label: Synapse
concepts.description: Junction between neurons where signals are transmitted

concepts.label: Action Potential
concepts.description: Electrical impulse that travels along a neuron's membrane

concepts.label: Neurotransmitter
concepts.description: Chemical messenger that transmits signals across synapses

concepts.label: Neural Plasticity
concepts.description: Brain's ability to reorganize structure and function in response to experience

concepts.label: Long-Term Potentiation
concepts.description: Persistent strengthening of synapses based on repeated stimulation

concepts.label: Long-Term Depression
concepts.description: Long-lasting decrease in synaptic strength

concepts.label: Cerebral Cortex
concepts.description: Outer layer of the brain responsible for higher cognitive functions

concepts.label: Hippocampus
concepts.description: Brain region critical for memory formation and spatial navigation

concepts.label: Amygdala
concepts.description: Brain region involved in emotion processing and fear responses

concepts.label: Prefrontal Cortex
concepts.description: Frontal region responsible for executive functions and decision making

concepts.label: Neurogenesis
concepts.description: Process of generating new neurons in the brain

concepts.label: Connectome
concepts.description: Comprehensive map of neural connections in the brain

concepts.label: Brain Rhythm
concepts.description: Oscillatory neural activity patterns at different frequencies

concepts.label: Brain-Computer Interface
concepts.description: Technology that enables direct communication between brain and external devices

concepts.label: Neuromodulation
concepts.description: Regulation of neural activity by diffuse neurotransmitter systems

concepts.label: Neuroimaging
concepts.description: Techniques for visualizing brain structure and activity

concepts.label: Glial Cell
concepts.description: Non-neuronal brain cells that support and protect neurons

concepts.label: Dendrite
concepts.description: Neuron branch that receives signals from other neurons

concepts.label: Axon
concepts.description: Neuron fiber that transmits signals to other neurons

concepts.label: Myelin
concepts.description: Insulating sheath around axons that speeds up signal transmission

concepts.label: Hebbian Learning
concepts.description: Neurons that fire together wire together

concepts.label: Homeostatic Plasticity
concepts.description: Mechanisms that maintain stable neural activity levels

concepts.label: Place Cell
concepts.description: Hippocampal neuron that fires at specific spatial locations

concepts.label: Mirror Neuron
concepts.description: Neuron that fires both during action and observation of same action

concepts.label: Neural Ensemble
concepts.description: Groups of neurons that cooperate to process information

concepts.label: Critical Period
concepts.description: Time window when brain is particularly sensitive to environmental input

concepts.label: Cortical Column
concepts.description: Vertical organization of neurons as functional units in cortex

concepts.label: Cognition
concepts.description: Mental processes of acquiring knowledge and understanding

concepts.label: Mental Representation
concepts.description: Internal symbolic or neural encoding of external reality

concepts.label: Working Memory
concepts.description: System for temporarily holding and manipulating information

concepts.label: Attention
concepts.description: Cognitive process of selectively focusing on relevant information

concepts.label: Executive Function
concepts.description: High-level cognitive processes controlling thought and action

concepts.label: Categorization
concepts.description: Organizing objects and ideas into meaningful groups

concepts.label: Concept Formation
concepts.description: Process of building abstract ideas from experience

concepts.label: Analogy
concepts.description: Mapping knowledge from familiar domains to novel ones

concepts.label: Abstraction
concepts.description: Extracting essential features while ignoring irrelevant details

concepts.label: Mental Model
concepts.description: Internal representation of how something works in the world

concepts.label: Metacognition
concepts.description: Awareness and understanding of one's own thought processes

concepts.label: Cognitive Load
concepts.description: Mental effort being used in working memory

concepts.label: Schema
concepts.description: Organized pattern of thought that helps interpret information

concepts.label: Prototype Theory
concepts.description: Categorization based on typical features rather than strict definitions

concepts.label: Decision Making
concepts.description: Cognitive process of selecting among alternatives

concepts.label: Cognitive Dissonance
concepts.description: Mental discomfort from holding contradictory beliefs

concepts.label: Language
concepts.description: System of symbols and rules for communication

concepts.label: Semantics
concepts.description: Study of meaning in language and representation

concepts.label: Embodied Cognition
concepts.description: Theory that cognition is shaped by body and environment

concepts.label: Distributed Cognition
concepts.description: Cognition extended across individuals, tools, and environment

concepts.label: Heuristic
concepts.description: Mental shortcut that enables quick decision making

concepts.label: Cognitive Bias
concepts.description: Systematic pattern of deviation from rational judgment

concepts.label: Predictive Coding
concepts.description: Brain continuously generates and updates predictions about sensory input

concepts.label: Bayesian Brain
concepts.description: Theory that brain uses Bayesian inference to process information

concepts.label: Supervised Learning
concepts.description: Learning from labeled training data with known outputs

concepts.label: Unsupervised Learning
concepts.description: Finding patterns in unlabeled data without predefined categories

concepts.label: Reinforcement Learning
concepts.description: Agent learns by interacting with environment and receiving rewards

concepts.label: Gradient Descent
concepts.description: Optimization algorithm that minimizes loss by following gradients

concepts.label: Backpropagation
concepts.description: Algorithm for computing gradients through neural network layers

concepts.label: Loss Function
concepts.description: Quantifies the difference between predicted and actual values

concepts.label: Overfitting
concepts.description: Model learns training data too well, fails to generalize

concepts.label: Underfitting
concepts.description: Model is too simple to capture underlying patterns in data

concepts.label: Regularization
concepts.description: Techniques to prevent overfitting by adding constraints

concepts.label: Cross-Validation
concepts.description: Technique for assessing model generalization on unseen data

concepts.label: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
concepts.description: Tradeoff between underfitting and overfitting in model complexity

concepts.label: Hyperparameter
concepts.description: Configuration parameter set before training begins

concepts.label: Learning Rate
concepts.description: Step size in gradient descent that controls update magnitude

concepts.label: Batch Normalization
concepts.description: Technique to normalize layer inputs for stable training

concepts.label: Dropout
concepts.description: Regularization by randomly dropping neurons during training

concepts.label: Early Stopping
concepts.description: Halting training when validation performance stops improving

concepts.label: Data Augmentation
concepts.description: Creating modified training data to improve generalization

concepts.label: Transfer Learning
concepts.description: Applying knowledge from one task to improve learning on another

concepts.label: Curse of Dimensionality
concepts.description: Problems arising from high-dimensional data spaces

concepts.label: Feature Engineering
concepts.description: Selecting and transforming variables to improve model performance

concepts.label: Embedding
concepts.description: Mapping discrete objects to continuous vector representations

concepts.label: Attention Mechanism
concepts.description: Technique allowing model to focus on relevant input parts

concepts.label: Transformer
concepts.description: Neural architecture based entirely on self-attention mechanisms

concepts.label: Convolution
concepts.description: Operation that applies filters to extract local patterns

concepts.label: Recurrent Network
concepts.description: Neural network with connections forming cycles for sequential data

concepts.label: Generative Model
concepts.description: Model that learns to generate new samples from data distribution

concepts.label: Autoencoder
concepts.description: Neural network that learns efficient data encodings unsupervised

concepts.label: Adversarial Training
concepts.description: Training with adversarial examples to improve robustness

concepts.label: Meta-Learning
concepts.description: Learning to learn, optimizing the learning process itself

concepts.label: Representation Learning
concepts.description: Automatically discovering useful data representations during training

concepts.label: Distributed Representation
concepts.description: Information encoded as patterns across many neurons or units

concepts.label: Emergence
concepts.description: Complex patterns arising from simple local interactions

concepts.label: Self-Organization
concepts.description: System spontaneously forms ordered patterns without central control

concepts.label: Nonlinear Dynamics
concepts.description: Systems where output is not proportional to input

concepts.label: Feedback Loop
concepts.description: Output of system feeds back as input, amplifying or stabilizing behavior

concepts.label: Attractor
concepts.description: State toward which a dynamical system evolves over time

concepts.label: Chaos Theory
concepts.description: Study of deterministic systems sensitive to initial conditions

concepts.label: Butterfly Effect
concepts.description: Small changes in initial conditions lead to large differences in outcome

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.description: Sudden qualitative change in system behavior at a critical point

concepts.label: Criticality
concepts.description: System poised at boundary between order and chaos

concepts.label: Tipping Point
concepts.description: Threshold beyond which a system undergoes rapid change

concepts.label: Scale-Free Network
concepts.description: Network where few nodes have many connections, most have few

concepts.label: Small-World Network
concepts.description: Network with high clustering and short path lengths between nodes

concepts.label: Power Law
concepts.description: Mathematical relationship where one quantity varies as power of another

concepts.label: Fractal
concepts.description: Pattern that repeats at different scales of magnification

concepts.label: Self-Similarity
concepts.description: Property where structure repeats across different scales

concepts.label: Agent-Based Model
concepts.description: Simulation of autonomous agents to study emergent behavior

concepts.label: Complex Adaptive System
concepts.description: System where agents adapt behavior based on interactions

concepts.label: Edge of Chaos
concepts.description: Transition zone where systems are most adaptable and creative

concepts.label: Coevolution
concepts.description: Mutual evolution where changes in one entity affect another

concepts.label: Network Theory
concepts.description: Study of interconnected systems through graph representations

concepts.label: Robustness
concepts.description: System's ability to maintain function despite perturbations

concepts.label: Resilience
concepts.description: Capacity to recover and adapt after disturbance

concepts.label: Path Dependence
concepts.description: Outcomes shaped by historical sequence of decisions

concepts.label: System
concepts.description: Set of interconnected elements forming a unified whole

concepts.label: Holism
concepts.description: Whole is more than sum of its parts

concepts.label: Homeostasis
concepts.description: System's ability to maintain stable internal conditions

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.description: Measure of disorder or uncertainty in a system

concepts.label: Negentropy
concepts.description: Measure of order, organization, and information in a system

concepts.label: Open System
concepts.description: System that exchanges energy and matter with its environment

concepts.label: Closed System
concepts.description: System that exchanges energy but not matter with environment

concepts.label: Feedback Control
concepts.description: Regulation of system output by using feedback for correction

concepts.label: Positive Feedback
concepts.description: Feedback that amplifies change, driving system away from equilibrium

concepts.label: Negative Feedback
concepts.description: Feedback that counteracts change, maintaining stability

concepts.label: Equilibrium
concepts.description: State where opposing forces in system are balanced

concepts.label: Dynamic Equilibrium
concepts.description: State of balance maintained through continuous change

concepts.label: Emergence
concepts.description: Novel properties arising from system component interactions

concepts.label: Hierarchy Theory
concepts.description: Systems organized in nested levels with distinct properties at each

concepts.label: Subsystem
concepts.description: Component system within a larger system

concepts.label: Supersystem
concepts.description: Larger system within which a system is embedded

concepts.label: Coupling
concepts.description: Degree of interdependence between system components

concepts.label: Requisite Variety
concepts.description: System's internal diversity must match environmental complexity

concepts.label: Cybernetics
concepts.description: Study of control and communication in systems

concepts.label: Second-Order Cybernetics
concepts.description: Cybernetics of observing systems, including the observer

concepts.label: Autopoiesis
concepts.description: System capable of self-producing and maintaining its own organization

concepts.label: Redundancy
concepts.description: Duplication of components for reliability and robustness

concepts.label: Modularity
concepts.description: Degree to which system components can be separated and recombined

concepts.label: Adaptation
concepts.description: System's ability to adjust behavior in response to environment

concepts.label: Information
concepts.description: Reduction of uncertainty, meaningful data in system communication

concepts.label: Control Parameter
concepts.description: Variable that determines system behavior near transition points

concepts.label: State Space
concepts.description: Set of all possible states a system can occupy

concepts.label: Self-Reference
concepts.description: System referring to itself, creating circular causality
